items.headline: NBA Game 6 Highlights: Timberwolves 110, Nuggets 98
items.blurb: NBA Game 6 Highlights: Timberwolves 110, Nuggets 98 — Jaden McDaniels dropped a game-high 32 points and added 10 rebounds, Terrence Shannon Jr. had 24 points, and the Minnesota Timberwolves took down the Denver Nuggets with a 110-98 win in Game 6.

items.headline: Timberwolves eliminate Nuggets after thrilling Game 6 victory
items.blurb: Timberwolves eliminate Nuggets after thrilling Game 6 victory — Jaden McDaniels had 32 points and 10 rebounds and Terrence Shannon Jr. scored 24 points in a surprise start for the injury-ravaged Minnesota Timberwolves in a 110-98 victory over the Denver Nuggets in Game 6 on Thursday night that finished off another tense NBA playoff series between the rivals.

items.headline: Celtics stay ice-cold from three-point range in Game 6 loss to 76ers
items.blurb: Celtics stay ice-cold from three-point range in Game 6 loss to 76ers — For the last few seasons, the Boston Celtics have built an elite offense by overwhelming opponents with three-point shooting and ball movement. When the shots aren't falling, like they weren't in Thursday's Game 6 loss, that offense looks very ordinary.

items.headline: Tempers flare in Knicks-Hawks Game 6 blowout as multiple players are ejected
items.blurb: Tempers flare in Knicks-Hawks Game 6 blowout as multiple players are ejected — Facing elimination at home down 3-2 in their first-round series, the Atlanta Hawks didn't show much fight. Until guard Dyson Daniels started an actual fight after the game was out of hand.

items.headline: Maxey sparks the 76ers’ Game 6 surge, force Game 7 vs. Celtics
items.blurb: Maxey sparks the 76ers’ Game 6 surge, force Game 7 vs. Celtics — Tyrese Maxey scored 30 points, a resurgent Paul George had 23 points and a Philadelphia 76ers’ team that lost by 32 points twice in this first-round playoff series played their most complete game of the season and forced Game 7 with a 106-93 win over the Boston Celtics on Thursday night.

items.headline: NBA Game 6 Highlights: 76ers 106, Celtics 93
items.blurb: NBA Game 6 Highlights: 76ers 106, Celtics 93 — Tyrese Maxey led the way with 30 points, Joel Embiid put up a near triple-double with a 19-10-8 line, and the Philadelphia 76ers forced Game 7 with a 106-93 win over the Boston Celtics.

items.headline: Moussa Diabate Wins 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award
items.blurb: Moussa Diabate Wins 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award — Charlotte Hornets center Moussa Diabate has won the 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award. The honor recognizes players whose effort-based contributions drive winning but rarely appear in traditional statistical categories. items.headline: NBA Game 6 Highlights: Knicks 140, Hawks 89
items.blurb: NBA Game 6 Highlights: Knicks 140, Hawks 89 — O.G. Anunoby led the way with 29 points and Karl-Anthony Towns tallied a triple-double as the New York Knicks obliterated the Atlanta Hawks 140-89 to close out their first-round series.

items.blurb: NBA Urges Federal Regulator To Tighten Prediction Market Rules To Protect Game Integrity — The NBA formally asked the Commodity Futures Trading Commission on Thursday to impose stricter regulations on prediction markets, citing risks to competitive integrity and public confidence in professional sports. items.blurb: Hawks Plan On Retaining CJ McCollum In Free Agency — CJ McCollum has had a career renaissance with the Atlanta Hawks as he is set to enter free agency this offseason. items.blurb: Lakers Relocate G League Team To Coachella Valley — The Los Angeles Lakers are relocating their NBA G League team to the Greater Palm Springs region in California, where it will be renamed the Coachella Valley Lakers. items.blurb: Rockets Avoid Elimination Again, Force Game 6 Against Lakers — The Houston Rockets avoided elimination Wednesday night, defeating the Los Angeles Lakers 99-93 in Game 5 to cut the first-round series deficit to 3-2. items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Flirts with triple-double — Embiid produced 19 points (6-18 FG, 1-5 3Pt, 6-7 FT), 10 rebounds, eight assists and one steal over 34 minutes during Thursday's 106-93 victory over the Celtics in Game 6 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Kevin Durant To Miss Game 6 Against Lakers With Ankle Injury — Houston Rockets forward Kevin Durant will remain sidelined for Game 6 of his team's first-round playoff series against the Los Angeles Lakers with an ankle injury.
